examples: Igneous and metamorphic rocks are more compact, commonly crystalline, and rarely contain spaces between grains.
Diamonds, an occasional component of rare igneous rocks called lamproites and kimberlites, have never been dated satisfactorily.
Some very ancient sediments were no longer recognizable, having been converted to igneous and metamorphic rocks in the course of mountain building.
In particular, biological materials alter igneous rocks in well-understood ways.
